title: I’m aiming to break the $1M mark this year and push for $3M within the next three years. Let’s talk strategy.
author: CulturalMeat9504
selftext:
Just checked my Robinhood year-end report—wrapped up the year with a 35.8% return, bringing the account to about $638k.



I’m not posting this to flex, but to talk about the mechanics of how I trade using ICT Liquidity concepts, and more importantly, why sitting on your hands is the hardest but most essential part of this game.



I stick to one simple model. Here is the breakdown:

Filter out the noise

Stop looking at every random signal in the middle of the range. I only care about two levels: the Previous Day High (PDH) and the Previous Day Low (PDL). This is where retail stop-losses are clustered. Large institutions need to hunt these pools of liquidity to fill their massive orders.



Identify the Trap (The Liquidity Sweep)

When price breaks above the PDH, most people think it is a breakout and time to buy. Usually, that is a trap—a Liquidity Sweep. The market makers push price up to bait breakout buyers and hit the stops of early short sellers. This surge in buying pressure is exactly what institutions use to sell their own positions at a premium.



Wait for Evidence (MSS and FVG)

A sweep alone isn't enough to enter. You need confirmation. I wait for a violent reversal that snaps through the previous support—a Market Structure Shift (MSS). This aggressive move leaves behind a Fair Value Gap (FVG). That gap is the footprint left by institutional money.



Execute like a sniper

Never chase the dump. Wait for a weak retracement back into that FVG. That is your entry. By entering there, your stop-loss goes right above the sweep high. This gives you tight risk with massive profit potential.



Discipline is everything

If you look at my screenshot, you will see I am currently holding nearly $590k in cash. The market has been choppy lately, and in the world of ICT, "No Sweep, No Trade." If the setup isn't crystal clear, I would much rather sit on my cash and wait.



Instead of scalping every minor move, I prefer waiting for the moments where retail is in despair and the big money has made its move.



Hope this gives some of you more clarity on your own systems. Stay disciplined, and let's all hit our goals this year. Cheers! I'd love to connect with like-minded traders so we can exchange ideas and learn from each other—feel free to reach out!
